# Code RED Decompiled Source Export
# Source: docs/REPO_STRUCTURE.md
# SHA1: f85d03106fe01d4cc7b369dc3c0ed64b5c2f064b
# Export type: source/text copy

# Code RED Repository Structure

This document defines the intended layout so Code RED does not drift into broken cleanup labels, duplicate launchers, or mixed generated outputs.

## Active root launchers

Keep these at the repo root because they are intended user entry points:

```text
Run_Code_RED.bat
Run_CodeRED_AI_Menu_Setup.bat
Run_CodeRED_Build_Assistant.bat
Run_CodeRED_Repo_Doctor.bat
Code_RED_Dependency_Installer.bat
```

Do not add a new root launcher for every research lane. Prefer adding new actions inside the workbench, build assistant, tuner, or a related app folder.

## Active runtime folders

These names are active runtime paths and must not be renamed:

```text
data/
logs/
related_apps/
tools/
```

Do not rename them to cleanup labels like:

```text
data - Important/
logs - Organize/
related_apps - Combine/
tools - Implement/
```

Those labels may look tidy, but they break launchers and tools that expect the original paths.

## Main app and workbench

```text
main.py
python_workbench.py
requirements.txt
```

These are the primary Code RED workbench entry files.

## Active tools

```text
tools/codered_actor_enum_tool.py
tools/codered_build_assistant.py
tools/codered_repo_doctor.py
```

Other helper tools can live in `tools/`, but generated logs, caches, and one-off outputs should not be committed there.

## Runtime data

```text
data/codered/
```

Active data includes actor enum maps, NPC rosters, behavior action menus, trainer/AI profiles, and related runtime files. Keep this folder available for the ASI/menu and setup tools.

## Related apps

```text
related_apps/
```

This folder contains the active and research-adjacent Code RED lanes, including:

- CodeRED Tuner
- Code Red Arcade demo
- ScriptHookRDR AI Menu
- Faction War research and builder lanes
- MP Companion lanes
- WSI/WGD/refgroup/navres research tools
- vehicle and content patch research

Do not delete or archive related-app folders without a no-regression manifest.

## Faction War lane

```text
related_apps/CodeRedFactionWar/
related_apps/CodeRedFactionWar/research_notes/
```

Faction War research notes and pass-planning files should stay close to the related app, especially Pass 16 living-world notes.

Large generated RPF outputs should stay out of GitHub unless intentionally published as a release artifact.

## Logs

Use `logs/` for curated pass reports, validation reports, handoffs, and project index files.

Prefer committing:

```text
logs/CodeRED_*_Pass*.md
logs/CodeRED_LOG_INDEX.md
logs/README.md
```

Avoid committing disposable runtime logs unless they are useful proof files:

```text
logs/CodeRED_Build_Assistant_last.log
logs/CodeRED_Build_Assistant_last_report.json
logs/CodeRED_Repo_Doctor_Report.json
```

## Generated outputs and caches

Do not commit generated build/cache clutter:

```text
__pycache__/
*.pyc
*.pdb
*.obj
*.pch
*.ipdb
*.iobj
*.vc.db
.vs/
build/
dist/
related_apps/Code_RED_ScriptHookRDR_AI_Menu/build/
CodeRED_Backups/
```

## New work rule

Before adding a file, choose one bucket:

```text
active runtime source
active tool
runtime data
curated log/report
related-app research
release artifact
local/generated output
```

If it is local/generated output, keep it out of Git unless it is intentionally preserved as proof.
